description: CodingAgent canonical benchmark suite — 10 representative coding tasks covering creation, editing, debugging, and refactoring.

scenarios.name: hello_world_function
scenarios.description: Create a simple function that returns a greeting string
scenarios.task: Create a file called greet.py with a function greet(name) that returns 'Hello, <name>!'
scenarios.test_command: python -c "from greet import greet; assert greet('World') == 'Hello, World!', f'Got: {greet(\"World\")}'"

scenarios.name: class_with_methods
scenarios.description: Create a class with initializer and methods
scenarios.task: Create a file counter.py with a Counter class. It should have an __init__(self, start=0) method and increment(), decrement(), and value() methods.
scenarios.test_command: python -c "from counter import Counter; c = Counter(); c.increment(); c.increment(); assert c.value() == 2; c.decrement(); assert c.value() == 1"

scenarios.name: fix_syntax_error
scenarios.description: Fix a syntax error in existing code
scenarios.task: Fix the syntax error in buggy.py so it runs correctly

setup_files.buggy.py:
def add(a, b)
    return a + b

scenarios.test_command: python -c "from buggy import add; assert add(2, 3) == 5"

scenarios.name: add_unit_tests
scenarios.description: Write unit tests for an existing module
scenarios.task: Write pytest unit tests for the math_utils module in math_utils.py. Create test_math_utils.py with at least 3 test functions.

scenarios.name: refactor_extract_function
scenarios.description: Refactor duplicated code into a reusable function
scenarios.task: Refactor report.py to extract the repeated date-formatting logic into a helper function format_date(dt). The function should format a datetime as 'YYYY-MM-DD'.

setup_files.report.py:
from datetime import datetime

def report_start(dt):
    formatted = f"{dt.year:04d}-{dt.month:02d}-{dt.day:02d}"
    return f"Report started: {formatted}"

def report_end(dt):
    formatted = f"{dt.year:04d}-{dt.month:02d}-{dt.day:02d}"
    return f"Report ended: {formatted}"

scenarios.test_command: python -c "from report import report_start, report_end, format_date; from datetime import datetime; dt = datetime(2024, 3, 15); assert format_date(dt) == '2024-03-15'; assert 'Report started: 2024-03-15' in report_start(dt)"

scenarios.name: implement_stack
scenarios.task: Create stack.py implementing a Stack class with push(item), pop() (raises IndexError when empty), peek(), is_empty(), and size() methods.
scenarios.test_command: python -c "from stack import Stack; s = Stack(); assert s.is_empty(); s.push(1); s.push(2); assert s.size() == 2; assert s.peek() == 2; assert s.pop() == 2; assert s.size() == 1"

scenarios.name: fix_off_by_one
scenarios.description: Fix an off-by-one error in a loop
scenarios.task: Fix the off-by-one bug in search.py's binary_search function so it correctly returns the index of the target or -1 if not found.
scenarios.test_command: python -c "from search import binary_search; arr = [1,3,5,7,9,11]; assert binary_search(arr, 7) == 3; assert binary_search(arr, 11) == 5; assert binary_search(arr, 4) == -1"

scenarios.name: add_type_hints
scenarios.description: Add Python type hints to an existing module
scenarios.task: Add Python type hints to all functions in typed_utils.py. Use the typing module where needed.
scenarios.test_command: python -c "import ast, pathlib; src = pathlib.Path('typed_utils.py').read_text(); tree = ast.parse(src); hints = [n for n in ast.walk(tree) if isinstance(n, ast.AnnAssign) or (isinstance(n, ast.FunctionDef) and n.returns)]; assert len(hints) >= 1, 'No type annotations found'"

scenarios.name: create_cli_tool
scenarios.description: Create a simple CLI tool using argparse
scenarios.task: Create wordcount.py — a CLI tool that accepts a filename argument and prints the number of lines, words, and characters in the file. Use argparse.

setup_files.sample.txt:
Hello world
This is a test
Three lines here

scenarios.name: write_file_processor
scenarios.description: Create a multi-function file processing module
scenarios.task: Create file_processor.py with three functions: read_csv(path) that returns a list of dicts from a CSV file, filter_rows(rows, key, value) that returns rows where rows[key]==value, and write_json(data, path) that writes data as pretty-printed JSON.
scenarios.test_command: python -c "from file_processor import read_csv, filter_rows, write_json; rows = read_csv('data.csv'); assert len(rows) == 3; london = filter_rows(rows, 'city', 'London'); assert len(london) == 2; write_json(london, 'out.json'); import json; data = json.loads(open('out.json').read()); assert len(data) == 2"
